data.content:
Here's my analysis of the legacy patterns and the modernization plan:

**Legacy patterns identified:**

| # | Legacy Pattern | SDK-style Replacement |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Old `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` + manual `Import` | `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `<Compile Include="..." />` for each file | Auto-globbing (implicit) |
| 3 | Explicit `<Reference>` for BCL assemblies (System, System.Core, etc.) | Implicit with framework targeting |
| 4 | Separate Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` conditions | All shown values are SDK defaults |
| 5 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | MSBuild properties in `.csproj` (auto-generated) |
| 6 | `TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2 | `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 7 | `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, boilerplate | Not needed in SDK-style |

Now I'll apply the modernization:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize it.

**Legacy patterns found:**

1. **Old-style project format** — uses `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, and explicit `Import` statements for `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets`. SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`.

2. **Explicit `<Compile>` items** — SDK-style auto-includes `*.cs` files via globbing.

3. **Explicit framework references** (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) — These are implicit in SDK-style projects targeting .NET Framework.
   
4. **Debug/Release configurations and assembly metadata** — The separate PropertyGroups and `AssemblyInfo.cs` file are all handled automatically in SDK-style, where assembly attributes can be expressed directly as MSBuild properties.

5. **Framework targeting** — The `TargetFrameworkVersion` of v4.7.2 needs to become `TargetFramework` with `net472`, though this could be modernized to `net8.0` if moving to current .NET.

6. **Unnecessary metadata** — `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, and other legacy properties aren't needed in SDK-style projects.

Now I'm going to convert this to an SDK-style project file targeting `net472` and remove the separate `AssemblyInfo.cs` file since those properties will live in the `.csproj` instead.

<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 14 lines**. Here's what was removed/simplified:

- **Deleted:** `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` — metadata now lives as `<Description>`, `<Company>`, `<Copyright>`, `<Version>` properties (auto-generated at build time).
- **Removed:** Explicit `<Compile>` items — SDK-style auto-globs all `*.cs` files.
- **Removed:** BCL `<Reference>` items — implicit with `net472` targeting.
- **Removed:** Debug/Release property groups — all values were defaults.
- **Removed:** `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Configuration`/`Platform` conditions, manual `Import` statements — all unnecessary boilerplate in SDK-style.
- **Replaced:** `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>`.
